title: Owned Inspection, Ordering, Release, and Collection Integrity · Failure Repair Bay
summary: A preserved Go Pokedex run contains one plausible command, parsing, URL, JSON, client, cache, ownership, goroutine, cancellation, persistence, security, test, packaging, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in offline inspectable and reversible collection interface and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal de…
objectives: Distinguish owned inspection from remote lookup and return a clear not-owned result without hidden traffic.
Present collection records in a documented stable order with identifiers and totals independent of map iteration.
Validate target, preview the exact owned record, require deliberate confirmation policy, then commit deletion atomically.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

Order the Pokedex Go Collection Sequence Tests Pokedex product engineering and Go HTTP evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

For Pokedex product engineering and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Pokedex Go Collection Sequenc…, keep Pokedex Go Collection Sequence Tests connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: A debug Go team handles case 6658 during an accessible regional field-guide session. Build deterministic Go evidence to verify offline owned inspection, stable listing, no hidden fetch, and confirmed release; all goroutines, bodies, signals, timers, and files require named cleanup ownership; then inject one redirect, control sequence, file, timeout, or persistence failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Run catch, duplicate, inspect, list, release, cancel, and retry sequences while checking counts and identities after every step.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Pokedex Go Collection Sequence Tests has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
